16: package com.pinterest.singer.client.logback;
17: 
18: import com.pinterest.singer.thrift.LogMessage;
19: 
20: import ch.qos.logback.core.Appender;
21: import ch.qos.logback.core.Context;
22: import ch.qos.logback.core.encoder.EncoderBase;
23: import ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.RollingFileAppender;
24: import ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.SizeAndTimeBasedFNATP;
25: import ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.TimeBasedRollingPolicy;
26: import ch.qos.logback.core.util.FileSize;
27: import org.apache.thrift.TException;
28: import org.apache.thrift.protocol.TBinaryProtocol;
29: import org.apache.thrift.protocol.TProtocol;
30: import org.apache.thrift.transport.TFastFramedTransport;
31: import org.apache.thrift.transport.TIOStreamTransport;
32: import org.apache.thrift.transport.TTransport;
33: 
34: import java.io.File;
35: import java.io.IOException;
36: import java.io.OutputStream;
37: 
38: /**
39:  * Utils to create logback appenders
40:  */
41: public class AppenderUtils {
42: 
43:   public static final String PATH_SEP = "/";
44: 
45:   private AppenderUtils() {
46:   }
47: 
48:   /**
49:    * Encoder for LogMessage objects.
50:    */
51:   public static class LogMessageEncoder extends EncoderBase<LogMessage> {
52: 
53:     private TTransport framedTransport;
54:     private TProtocol protocol;
55:     private OutputStream os;
56: 
57:     @Override
58:     public void init(OutputStream os) {
59:       this.os = os;
60:       // Use the TFlushingFastFramedTransport to be compatible with singer_thrift
61:       // log.
62:       final int bufferCapacity = 10;
63:       framedTransport = new TFastFramedTransport(new TIOStreamTransport(os),
64:           bufferCapacity);
65:       protocol = new TBinaryProtocol(framedTransport);
66:     }
67: 
68:     @Override
69:     public void doEncode(LogMessage logMessage) throws IOException {
70:       try {
71:         logMessage.write(protocol);
72:         framedTransport.flush();
73:       } catch (TException e) {
74:         throw new IOException(e);
75:       }
76:     }
77: 
78:     @Override
79:     public void close() throws IOException {
80:       framedTransport.close();
81:     }
82:   }
83: 
84:   /**
85:    * Create the basic thrift appender which logs to a file
86:    * and rolls the file when it exceeds a certain size.
87:    *
88:    * @param basePath base directory the files are under.
89:    * @param topic the topic name for the current appender.
90:    * @param rotateThresholdKBytes threshold in kilobytes to rotate after.
91:    * @param context the logback context.
92:    */
93:   public static Appender<LogMessage> createFileRollingThriftAppender(
94:       File basePath,
95:       String topic,
96:       long rotateThresholdKBytes,
97:       Context context,
98:       int maxRetentionHours) {
99:     RollingFileAppender<LogMessage> appender = new RollingFileAppender<LogMessage>();
100:     appender.setContext(context);
101:     appender.setAppend(true);
102:     appender.setPrudent(false);
103: 
104:     LogMessageEncoder encoder = new LogMessageEncoder();
105:     appender.setEncoder(encoder);
106:     appender.setFile(basePath + PATH_SEP + topic);
107: 
108:     TimeBasedRollingPolicy policy = new TimeBasedRollingPolicy();
109:     policy.setMaxHistory(maxRetentionHours);
110:     policy.setFileNamePattern(basePath + PATH_SEP + topic + ".%d{yyyy-MM-dd-HH}.%i");
111:     policy.setCleanHistoryOnStart(false);
112:     policy.setContext(context);
113:     policy.setParent(appender);
114: 
115:     // Also impose a max size per file policy.
116:     SizeAndTimeBasedFNATP fnatp = new SizeAndTimeBasedFNATP();
117:     fnatp.setContext(context);
118:     fnatp.setTimeBasedRollingPolicy(policy);
119:     fnatp.setMaxFileSize(FileSize.valueOf(String.format("%sKB", rotateThresholdKBytes)));
120: 
121:     policy.setTimeBasedFileNamingAndTriggeringPolicy(fnatp);
122:     appender.setRollingPolicy(policy);
123:     appender.setTriggeringPolicy(policy);
124: 
125:     policy.start();
126:     appender.start();
127: 
128:     return appender;
129:   }
130: }

(End of file - total 130 lines)

<path>/workspace/singer/thrift-logger/src/main/java/com/pinterest/singer/client/logback/AppenderUtils.java</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: /**
2:  * Copyright 2019 Pinterest, Inc.
3:  *
4:  * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5:  * you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6:  * You may obtain a copy of the License at
7:  *
8:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9:  *
10:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14:  * limitations under the License.
15:  */
16: package com.pinterest.singer.client.logback;
17: 
18: import com.pinterest.singer.thrift.LogMessage;
19: import ch.qos.logback.core.Appender;
20: import ch.qos.logback.core.Context;
21: import ch.qos.logback.core.encoder.EncoderBase;
22: import ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.RollingFileAppender;
23: import ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.SizeAndTimeBasedFNATP;
24: import ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.TimeBasedRollingPolicy;
25: import ch.qos.logback.core.util.FileSize;
26: import org.apache.thrift.TException;
27: import org.apache.thrift.protocol.TBinaryProtocol;
28: import org.apache.thrift.protocol.TProtocol;
29: import org.apache.thrift.transport.TFastFramedTransport;
30: import org.apache.thrift.transport.TIOStreamTransport;
31: import org.apache.thrift.transport.TTransport;
32: import java.io.File;
33: import java.io.IOException;
34: import java.io.OutputStream;
35: 
36: /**
37:  * Utils to create logback appenders
38:  */
39: public class AppenderUtils {
40: 
41:     public static final String PATH_SEP = "/";
42: 
43:     private AppenderUtils() {
44:     }
45: 
46:     /**
47:      * Encoder for LogMessage objects.
48:      */
49:     public static class LogMessageEncoder extends EncoderBase<LogMessage> {
50: 
51:         private TTransport framedTransport;
52: 
53:         private TProtocol protocol;
54: 
55:         private OutputStream os;
56: 
57:         @Override
58:         public void init(OutputStream os) {
59:             this.os = os;
60:             // Use the TFlushingFastFramedTransport to be compatible with singer_thrift
61:             // log.
62:             final int bufferCapacity = 10;
63:             framedTransport = new TFastFramedTransport(new TIOStreamTransport(os), bufferCapacity);
64:             protocol = new TBinaryProtocol(framedTransport);
65:         }
66: 
67:         @Override
68:         public void doEncode(LogMessage logMessage) throws IOException {
69:             try {
70:                 logMessage.write(protocol);
71:                 framedTransport.flush();
72:             } catch (TException e) {
73:                 throw new IOException(e);
74:             }
75:         }
76: 
77:         @Override
78:         public void close() throws IOException {
79:             framedTransport.close();
80:         }
81: 
82:         @Override
83:         public byte[] footerBytes() {
84:             return {};
85:         }
86:     }
87: 
88:     /**
89:      * Create the basic thrift appender which logs to a file
90:      * and rolls the file when it exceeds a certain size.
91:      *
92:      * @param basePath base directory the files are under.
93:      * @param topic the topic name for the current appender.
94:      * @param rotateThresholdKBytes threshold in kilobytes to rotate after.
95:      * @param context the logback context.
96:      */
97:     public static Appender<LogMessage> createFileRollingThriftAppender(File basePath, String topic, long rotateThresholdKBytes, Context context, int maxRetentionHours) {
98:         RollingFileAppender<LogMessage> appender = new RollingFileAppender<LogMessage>();
99:         appender.setContext(context);
100:         appender.setAppend(true);
101:         appender.setPrudent(false);
102:         LogMessageEncoder encoder = new LogMessageEncoder();
103:         appender.setEncoder(encoder);
104:         appender.setFile(basePath + PATH_SEP + topic);
105:         TimeBasedRollingPolicy policy = new TimeBasedRollingPolicy();
106:         policy.setMaxHistory(maxRetentionHours);
107:         policy.setFileNamePattern(basePath + PATH_SEP + topic + ".%d{yyyy-MM-dd-HH}.%i");
108:         policy.setCleanHistoryOnStart(false);
109:         policy.setContext(context);
110:         policy.setParent(appender);
111:         // Also impose a max size per file policy.
112:         SizeAndTimeBasedFNATP fnatp = new SizeAndTimeBasedFNATP();
113:         fnatp.setContext(context);
114:         fnatp.setTimeBasedRollingPolicy(policy);
115:         fnatp.setMaxFileSize(FileSize.valueOf(String.format("%sKB", rotateThresholdKBytes)));
116:         policy.setTimeBasedFileNamingAndTriggeringPolicy(fnatp);
117:         appender.setRollingPolicy(policy);
118:         appender.setTriggeringPolicy(policy);
119:         policy.start();
120:         appender.start();
121:         return appender;
122:     }
123: }

(End of file - total 123 lines)
</content>

There's a more fundamental issue - the `Encoder` interface has changed and now requires a different method signature. The `doEncode` method should be `encode` instead. This is a more significant API change in logback.

Looking at the errors:
1. `LogMessageEncoder` does not override abstract method `encode(LogMessage)` - the method name changed from `doEncode` to `encode`
2. The `@Override` annotations on `init`, `doEncode`, and `close` are failing because the method signatures have changed

This is actually a more complex issue than just adding `footerBytes()`. The logback Encoder interface has changed significantly between versions. I need to update the transformation to fix all these issues.

Let me update the transformation to:
1. Rename `doEncode` to `encode`

- Add `footerBytes()` method returning `null`
